source_name: European Banking Authority
title: The EBA consults on amendments to the RTS on the assignment of risk weights to specialised lending exposures under the Supervisory Slotting Criteria Approach
clean_title: The EBA consults on amendments to the RTS on the assignment of risk weights to specialised lending exposures under the Supervisory Slotting Criteria Approach
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today launched a public consultation on proposed amendments to its Regulatory Technical Standards (RTS), set out in a Delegated Regulation, on the assignment of risk weights to specialised lending exposures under the Supervisory Slotting Criteria Approach (SSCA). The purpose of the amendments is to update the RTS in light of the changes introduced by the revised Capital Requirements Regulation (CRR 3) and to enhance the risk sensitivity, clarity and usability of the framework. Overall, the…

source_name: European Banking Authority
title: The EBA amends Guidelines on the definition of default
clean_title: The EBA amends Guidelines on the definition of default
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) published today its final Report amending the Guidelines on the application of the definition of default. The Report introduces targeted amendments to better reflect specific aspects of non recourse factoring. It also confirms that the 1% threshold applied to reductions in net present value loss (NPV threshold) in debt restructuring remains appropriate for prudential default recognition.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
